purpose: Cross-document discourse-skeleton reuse — the QUDsim axis (spec 28, M1b; arXiv:2504.09373). Compares documents by their ORDERED discourse skeleton, not their words, with a model-free proxy: each discourse unit gets a content-word-free rhetorical-move signature (discourse-marker bucket x length-tercile x terminal-punctuation class), discretized to a symbol, forming an ordered skeleton string per document. Cross-document overlap is a normalized longest-common-subsequence ratio (stdlib difflib) over the symbol strings, yielding a descriptive {mean/median/max} pairwise overlap, a top-k pair table (with the shared aligned skeleton run), skeleton-similarity groups, and a readable per-document skeleton. Topic-robust by construction (content words excluded), so high overlap cannot be an artifact of a shared subject. The M1b skeleton is a PROXY; the true LLM-parsed QUD lens is the gated M2 --qud-lens model (fail-loud missing_dependency, POC-gated). NOT an AI/human or plagiarism determination: high overlap = shared discourse template, NOT 'AI' (a shared genre, a tight prompt, or a house style all raise it legitimately); measurements only, no band/verdict; --report-threshold only GROUPS for display; the claim-license refuses provenance / plagiarism / selection.
use_when: You want to read how much a CORPUS reuses the same discourse template (structural homogenization), independent of topic
You suspect lexically diverse but structurally identical documents (the "everything has the same bones" axis no per-document surface can see)
The corpus has >= --min-docs (default 3) documents, each a few sentences long
do_not_use_when: You want an AI-provenance call (high skeleton-overlap is NOT 'AI'; this is not an AI signal)
You want a plagiarism / derivative-work / copyright determination (it is a structural measurement)
The corpus is < --min-docs or a deliberately templated genre where shared structure is expected and not diagnostic (overlap is corpus- and register-dependent)

inputs.corpus: a --corpus-dir of texts OR a --manifest (JSONL; id + text|text_path)

compute.cost_note: stdlib only; no models for the default --qud-lens proxy (re/difflib/collections/statistics; sentence segmentation has a pure-regex fallback). --qud-lens model is a gated optional LLM lens (python_optional), off by default and fail-loud when its client is absent.

examples.description: Cross-document skeleton overlap across a corpus directory
Skeleton overlap from a manifest, grouping pairs at/above 0.85 for display

references: QUDsim — Quantifying Discourse Template Reuse in LLM-generated text (arXiv:2504.09373): https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09373
